---
title: "Fire_NOBO_Manuscript_2023 (reviewed)"
output: html_document
date: "2026-04-24"
editor_options:
  chunk_output_type: console
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

## Libraries

```{r}
library(janitor)
library(tidyverse)
library(lubridate)
library(readxl)
library(writexl)
library(betareg)
library(MuMIn)
library(performance)
theme_set(theme_minimal())
```

## Named constants

```{r}
BURN_DATE       <- ymd("2023-03-21")
FEED_START_DATE <- ymd("2023-06-06")
FEED_START_DSF  <- as.integer(FEED_START_DATE - BURN_DATE)  # 77
BOOT_B          <- 2000
BOUND_EPS       <- 1e-5
ACF_LAG         <- 7
```

## Helper functions

```{r}
bound_beta <- function(x, eps = BOUND_EPS) pmin(pmax(x, eps), 1 - eps)

boot_beta_ci <- function(formula, data, newdata, B = BOOT_B, seed = 123) {
  preds <- matrix(NA_real_, nrow = B, ncol = nrow(newdata))
  for (i in seq_len(B)) {
    set.seed(seed + i)
    d <- data[sample.int(nrow(data), replace = TRUE), ]
    fit <- tryCatch(
      suppressWarnings(betareg(formula, data = d, link = "logit")),
      error = function(e) NULL
    )
    if (!is.null(fit)) {
      preds[i, ] <- tryCatch(
        predict(fit, newdata = newdata, type = "response"),
        error = function(e) rep(NA_real_, nrow(newdata))
      )
    }
  }
  data.frame(
    lo = apply(preds, 2, quantile, probs = 0.025, na.rm = TRUE),
    hi = apply(preds, 2, quantile, probs = 0.975, na.rm = TRUE)
  )
}

boot_beta_ci_block <- function(formula, data, newdata, block_size = 7,
                               B = BOOT_B, seed = 123, time_var = "DSF") {
  data  <- data[order(data[[time_var]]), ]
  n     <- nrow(data)
  nblk  <- ceiling(n / block_size)
  starts_all <- seq_len(max(1, n - block_size + 1))
  preds <- matrix(NA_real_, nrow = B, ncol = nrow(newdata))
  for (i in seq_len(B)) {
    set.seed(seed + i)
    starts <- sample(starts_all, size = nblk, replace = TRUE)
    idx <- unlist(lapply(starts, function(s) s:min(s + block_size - 1, n)))
    idx <- idx[seq_len(n)]
    d   <- data[idx, ]
    fit <- tryCatch(
      suppressWarnings(betareg(formula, data = d, link = "logit")),
      error = function(e) NULL
    )
    if (!is.null(fit)) {
      preds[i, ] <- tryCatch(
        predict(fit, newdata = newdata, type = "response"),
        error = function(e) rep(NA_real_, nrow(newdata))
      )
    }
  }
  data.frame(
    lo = apply(preds, 2, quantile, probs = 0.025, na.rm = TRUE),
    hi = apply(preds, 2, quantile, probs = 0.975, na.rm = TRUE)
  )
}

check_autocorr <- function(model, label = "", lag = ACF_LAG) {
  r <- residuals(model, type = "pearson")
  cat("\n--- Autocorrelation diagnostics:", label, "---\n")
  print(Box.test(r, lag = lag, type = "Ljung-Box"))
  acf(r, main = paste("ACF of Pearson residuals:", label))
  invisible(r)
}

plot_theme <- theme(
  legend.position   = "none",
  panel.grid.major  = element_blank(),
  panel.grid.minor  = element_blank(),
  axis.line         = element_line("black"),
  axis.ticks        = element_line("black"),
  axis.text         = element_text(colour = "black", size = 12),
  axis.title        = element_text(colour = "black", size = 12),
  legend.title      = element_text(colour = "black", size = 12),
  legend.text       = element_text(colour = "black", size = 12),
  plot.margin       = margin(5, 10, 5, 5, "pt")
)
```

## Load data

```{r}
summer23 <- read_xlsx(
  "/Volumes/Samsung_T5/BOBWHITE_DATA/Clean/SummerLocations/Summer2023/s23rx_no6.xlsx"
) %>%
  mutate(date = ymd(date)) %>%
  filter(status == "A") %>%
  rename(DSF = dsf)

rain <- read_xlsx("/Users/grayum.jeffrey/Downloads/Crafton_rainfall_2023 .xlsx") %>%
  clean_names() %>%
  transmute(
    date          = ymd(date),
    rain_total_mm = rain_total_mm,
    dsf           = as.integer(date - BURN_DATE)
  )
```

## Build PIB

```{r}
bad_days <- summer23 %>%
  filter(is.na(birds_online) | birds_online == 0) %>%
  distinct(date, birds_online)
cat("Rows with birds_online NA or 0:", nrow(bad_days), "\n")
if (nrow(bad_days) > 0) print(bad_days)

birds_burned_daily <- summer23 %>%
  filter(burned == 1) %>%
  group_by(date) %>%
  summarise(birds_in_burned_areas = n_distinct(band_numb), .groups = "drop")

s23 <- summer23 %>%
  left_join(birds_burned_daily, by = "date") %>%
  mutate(birds_in_burned_areas = replace_na(birds_in_burned_areas, 0)) %>%
  filter(!is.na(birds_online), birds_online > 0) %>%
  mutate(pib = birds_in_burned_areas / birds_online)

stopifnot(all(is.finite(s23$pib)))
stopifnot(all(s23$pib >= 0 & s23$pib <= 1))
```

## Day-level dataset

```{r}
invariance <- s23 %>%
  group_by(DSF) %>%
  summarise(
    n_pib  = n_distinct(pib),
    n_hg   = n_distinct(hg),
    n_ps   = n_distinct(ps),
    n_week = n_distinct(week),
    .groups = "drop"
  )
violations <- filter(invariance, n_pib > 1 | n_hg > 1 | n_ps > 1 | n_week > 1)
if (nrow(violations) > 0) {
  warning("Within-day values vary on the following DSF; slice_head will silently pick one row:")
  print(violations)
} else {
  cat("Within-day invariance check: PASSED\n")
}

s23_daily <- s23 %>%
  arrange(DSF, date) %>%                     # deterministic slice_head
  group_by(DSF) %>%
  slice_head(n = 1) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  arrange(DSF) %>%                           # time order for ACF / Ljung-Box
  select(pib, DSF, week, start_date_of_week, hg:ps) %>%
  mutate(
    feed    = as.integer(DSF >= FEED_START_DSF),
    pib_raw = pib,
    pib     = bound_beta(pib)
  )

p0 <- mean(s23_daily$pib_raw == 0)
p1 <- mean(s23_daily$pib_raw == 1)
cat(sprintf("Proportion PIB == 0 (raw): %.3f\n", p0))
cat(sprintf("Proportion PIB == 1 (raw): %.3f\n", p1))
if (p0 > 0.05 || p1 > 0.05) {
  message("WARNING: >5% of days sit at a boundary. Consider a ZOIB model before trusting inference.")
}

ggplot(s23_daily, aes(DSF, pib_raw)) +
  geom_point() +
  geom_vline(xintercept = FEED_START_DSF, linetype = "dashed", color = "midnightblue") +
  labs(x = "Days since fire", y = "PIB", title = "2023") +
  plot_theme
```

## Rainfall joined (weekly totals; cumulative kept for descriptive plot)

```{r}
# Cumulative rainfall is retained only for descriptive visuals. For modeling
# we use weekly totals, because cum_rain_mm is monotonic in DSF (r ~ 0.97)
# and therefore a time proxy rather than a rainfall signal. Weekly rainfall
# is not monotonic, so any effect it carries is a genuine rainfall effect.
rain_cum_daily <- rain %>%
  arrange(dsf) %>%
  mutate(cum_rain_mm = cumsum(rain_total_mm)) %>%
  select(dsf, cum_rain_mm)

week_lookup_23 <- s23_daily %>%
  distinct(week, start_date_of_week) %>%
  arrange(start_date_of_week) %>%
  mutate(
    start_date = as.Date(start_date_of_week),
    end_date   = lead(start_date) - 1
  )
week_lookup_23$end_date[is.na(week_lookup_23$end_date)] <-
  week_lookup_23$start_date[is.na(week_lookup_23$end_date)] + 6

rain_weekly_23 <- rain %>%
  select(date, rain_total_mm) %>%
  tidyr::crossing(week_lookup_23) %>%
  filter(date >= start_date, date <= end_date) %>%
  group_by(week, start_date_of_week) %>%
  summarise(weekly_rain_mm = sum(rain_total_mm, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop")

s23_rain <- s23_daily %>%
  left_join(rain_cum_daily,  by = c("DSF" = "dsf")) %>%
  left_join(rain_weekly_23,  by = c("week", "start_date_of_week")) %>%
  arrange(DSF)

stopifnot(all(!is.na(s23_rain$cum_rain_mm)))
stopifnot(all(!is.na(s23_rain$weekly_rain_mm)))
```

## Collinearity audit (printed for the record)

```{r}
cor_mat <- s23_rain %>%
  select(ps, feed, weekly_rain_mm, DSF) %>%
  cor() %>%
  round(2)
cor_mat
```

## Beta-regression candidate set, ranked by AICc

```{r}
# Three univariate candidates plus two multivariate candidates combining the
# two predictors that are NOT collinear in 2022 (ps vs weekly_rain_mm at
# r = 0.26). Verify the 2023 cor_mat above shows a comparable low
# correlation before trusting the multivariate fits; if 2023 shows r > ~0.7
# between ps and weekly_rain_mm, drop both m_ps_rain and m_ps_x_rain from
# the set. DSF is excluded because it is structurally a time axis and
# correlates at r > 0.85 with ps and feed.
m_ps        <- betareg(pib ~ ps,                  data = s23_rain, link = "logit")
m_rain      <- betareg(pib ~ weekly_rain_mm,      data = s23_rain, link = "logit")
m_feed      <- betareg(pib ~ feed,                data = s23_rain, link = "logit")
m_ps_rain   <- betareg(pib ~ ps + weekly_rain_mm, data = s23_rain, link = "logit")
m_ps_x_rain <- betareg(pib ~ ps * weekly_rain_mm, data = s23_rain, link = "logit")

# Autocorrelation diagnostics on each candidate.
walk2(
  list(m_ps, m_rain, m_feed, m_ps_rain, m_ps_x_rain),
  c("ps", "weekly_rain_mm", "feed",
    "ps + weekly_rain_mm", "ps * weekly_rain_mm"),
  ~ check_autocorr(.x, label = .y)
)

# For single-predictor models we print the predictor's p-value; multivariate
# models get NA here because the table is single-slot. Full coefficient
# tables are printed below for each multivariate fit.
extract_fit <- function(model, name, p_row = 2) {
  sm <- summary(model)$coefficients$mean
  p  <- if (!is.na(p_row) && p_row <= nrow(sm)) sm[p_row, "Pr(>|z|)"] else NA_real_
  data.frame(
    Model = name,
    R2    = unname(performance::r2(model)$R2),
    AICc  = MuMIn::AICc(model),
    p     = p
  )
}

summary23_rain <- bind_rows(
  extract_fit(m_ps,        "PIB ~ ps"),
  extract_fit(m_rain,      "PIB ~ weekly_rain_mm"),
  extract_fit(m_feed,      "PIB ~ feed"),
  extract_fit(m_ps_rain,   "PIB ~ ps + weekly_rain_mm", p_row = NA),
  extract_fit(m_ps_x_rain, "PIB ~ ps * weekly_rain_mm", p_row = NA)
) %>%
  arrange(AICc) %>%
  mutate(
    deltaAICc = AICc - min(AICc),
    weight    = MuMIn::Weights(AICc)
  )

summary23_rain

# Coefficient summaries for the multivariate models so all slopes are visible.
cat("\n--- PIB ~ ps + weekly_rain_mm (2023) ---\n")
print(summary(m_ps_rain)$coefficients$mean)
cat("\n--- PIB ~ ps * weekly_rain_mm (2023) ---\n")
print(summary(m_ps_x_rain)$coefficients$mean)

write_xlsx(summary23_rain,
           "/Volumes/Samsung_T5/FIRE_NOBO_MANUSCRIPT/model_summary_23_rain_fixed.xlsx")
```


## Prediction plot: top model (PIB ~ feed)

```{r}
# feed is binary (0 = pre supp-feed, 1 = post). Predictions are two-level
# with bootstrap CIs, shown against jittered observed PIB by period.
m_top <- m_feed
summary(m_top)
check_autocorr(m_top, label = "top model: feed")

newdat <- data.frame(feed = c(0L, 1L))
newdat$pred <- predict(m_top, newdata = newdat, type = "response")

# Block-bootstrap CIs on the two-level prediction. Block size 7 days because
# daily PIB residuals are expected to be autocorrelated.
newdat <- cbind(
  newdat,
  boot_beta_ci_block(pib ~ feed,
                     s23_rain, newdat,
                     block_size = 7, B = BOOT_B, time_var = "DSF")
)

# Human-readable x-axis labels.
period_levels <- c("Pre supplemental feed", "Post supplemental feed")
newdat$period <- factor(period_levels[newdat$feed + 1L], levels = period_levels)

s23_rain_pts <- s23_rain %>%
  mutate(period = factor(period_levels[feed + 1L], levels = period_levels))

print(newdat)

ggplot() +
  geom_jitter(data = s23_rain_pts,
              aes(period, pib_raw),
              width = 0.12, height = 0, alpha = 0.7, size = 1.4, color = "red") +
  geom_errorbar(data = newdat,
                aes(period, ymin = lo, ymax = hi),
                width = 0.15, linewidth = 0.8, color = "black") +
  geom_point(data = newdat,
             aes(period, pred),
             size = 4, color = "black") +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 0.35)) +
  labs(x = NULL,
       y = "Predicted PIB",
       title = "2023") +
  theme_bw(base_family = "Times New Roman", base_size = 12) +
  theme(legend.position = "none",
        panel.grid.major = element_blank(),
        panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
        axis.line  = element_line("black"),
        axis.ticks = element_line("black"),
        axis.text.x  = element_text(colour = "black", size = 12),
        axis.title.y = element_text(colour = "black", size = 12),
        axis.title.x = element_text(colour = "black", size = 12),
        legend.title = element_text(colour = "black", size = 12),
        legend.text  = element_text(colour = "black", size = 12),
        axis.text.y  = element_text(colour = "black", size = 12),
        strip.background = element_blank(),
        strip.text = element_text(colour = "black", size = 12),
        plot.margin = margin(t = 5, r = 10, b = 5, l = 5, unit = "pt"))
```

```{r}
# Side-by-side PIB vs days-since-fire, 2022 | 2023.
# Assumes s22_daily and s23_daily are in scope from the two manuscript Rmds.

library(tidyverse)

both <- bind_rows(
  s22_daily %>% transmute(year = 2022, dsf = DSF, pib = pib_raw),
  s23_daily %>% transmute(year = 2023, dsf = DSF, pib = pib_raw)
)

vlines <- tibble(year = c(2022, 2023), psi = c(56, 77))  # supp-feed start DSF

ggplot(both, aes(dsf, pib)) +
  geom_vline(data = vlines, aes(xintercept = psi),
             linetype = "dashed", color = "midnightblue", linewidth = 0.5) +
  geom_point(size = 1.8, alpha = 0.9) +
  facet_wrap(~ year) +
  labs(x = "Days since fire", y = "PIB") +
  theme_bw(base_family = "Times New Roman", base_size = 12) +
  theme(legend.position = "none",
        panel.grid.major = element_blank(),
        panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
        axis.line  = element_line("black"),
        axis.ticks = element_line("black"),
        axis.text.x  = element_text(colour = "black", size = 12),
        axis.title.y = element_text(colour = "black", size = 12),
        axis.title.x = element_text(colour = "black", size = 12),
        axis.text.y  = element_text(colour = "black", size = 12),
        strip.background = element_blank(),
        strip.text = element_text(colour = "black", size = 12),
        plot.margin = margin(t = 5, r = 10, b = 5, l = 5, unit = "pt"))
```
